card.intuition: A styling approach where CSS is written using JavaScript (using libraries like Styled-components or Emotion). This tightly couples styles with logic and components.
card.analogy: Internal organs: instead of putting your skin (CSS) in a separate bag (CSS file), you grow it directly on your muscles (JavaScript components). It's incredibly convenient, but it makes the body heavier to move (runtime performance cost).
when_to_use: React/Next.js projects where styles depend heavily on dynamic props
Building UI libraries where styles should be self-contained in the component
Teams that prefer 'Colocation' (keeping CSS, HTML, and JS in one file)
gotchas: Runtime CSS-in-JS can cause 'Style Flicker' on initial load and slows down re-renders of large lists
Modern trends are moving towards 'Static' CSS-in-JS (like Panda CSS or Vanilla Extract) which have zero runtime cost

exercise.prompt: Identify the main performance disadvantage of using 'Runtime' CSS-in-JS libraries compared to plain CSS.
exercise.starter_code: tradeoff = "Main thread _______ overhead for style calculation."
hints: JS has to 'run' or 'execute' to generate the CSS
